title: TLS, Virtual Hosts, Credentials, and Authorization · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for tls, virtual hosts, credentials, and authorization and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go TLS and least-privilege connection contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Amqps Identity Verification · changed case · Quiz Amqps Identity Verification
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 1860 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify TLS and vhost identity, redact secrets, and compute least-privilege permissions; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then introduce a keyed sequence gap during single-active failover. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Tenant Isolation Abuse
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Tenant Isolation Abuse: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 7431 during bounded worker pool during a dependency slowdown. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify TLS and vhost identity, redact secrets, and compute least-privilege permissions; keep credentials and connection URLs out of output; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
